name: Ham Turnovers (Usda)
recipeIngredient: 2 1/2 pounds cooked ham, finely chopped (8 cups)
recipeInstructions:
1. FILLING: Mix ham, onion, tomato sauce, parsley flakes, pepper, mustard, and horseradish.
Set aside.
2. PASTRY: Mix flour and salt. Add shortening only until the mixture is crumbly.
Gradually add cold water and mix quickly. Let dough rest for 15 minutes.
3. Divide dough into 24 pieces. Form each piece into a ball. Roll out on a lightly
floured surface to make a circle about 5 inches in diameter, approximately 1/8-inch thick.
Place about 3 tablespoons of ham mixture onto one half of each pastry circle.
Fold the other half of the circle over the ham mixture. Seal the edge by pressing with the tines of a fork.
4. TO SERVE WITHOUT FREEZING:
- Preheat oven to 400°F (hot).
- Place turnovers on a baking sheet and bake for 30 minutes or until lightly browned.
- Serve with Mushroom Sauce or Vegetable Sauce.
5. TO FREEZE:
- Arrange six turnovers in a single layer on freezer wrap.
- Complete wrapping by pulling the paper up over the top of the food.
- Fold the edges of the wrap together several times so the paper lies directly on top of the food.
- Fold the ends of the freezer wrap over the top and seal with freezer tape.
- Label with the name of the food, date of freezing, and last date for best eating quality (about 6 months).
- Freeze immediately for 10 to 12 hours before stacking packages.
6. TO HEAT FROZEN TURNOVERS:
- Preheat oven to 400°F (hot).
- Remove freezer wrap.
- Place turnovers on a baking sheet and bake for 45 minutes or until lightly browned.
- Serve with Mushroom Sauce or Vegetable Sauce.
